title: haplophyllum dubium
intro: Haplophyllum dubium, also known as the 'Tribulus', is a perennial plant species native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions, characterized by its small yellow flowers and spiny stems. Traditionally, Haplophyllum dubium has been used in medicine due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including lowering blood pressure, improving digestion, and reducing the risk of certain cancers. Haplophyllum dubium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
